---
title: "Papers, February 2026"
bibliography: refs.bib
---

## [Massive Sound Embedding Benchmark (MSEB)](https://openreview.net/forum?id=X0juYgFVng)
- [Poster](https://neurips.cc/virtual/2025/loc/san-diego/poster/121597)
- [Code](https://github.com/google-research/mseb) --- Apache 2.0
- [SVQ dataset](https://huggingface.co/datasets/google/svq)
- [SVQ blog post](https://research.google/blog/speech-to-retrieval-s2r-a-new-approach-to-voice-search/)
- [Google Research](https://research.google/pubs/massive-sound-embedding-benchmark-mseb/)

## [MASSIVE: A 1M-Example Multilingual Natural Language Understanding Dataset with 51 Typologically-Diverse Languages](https://aclanthology.org/2023.acl-long.235/) [@fitzgerald-etal-2023-massive]
- Cited (indirectly) by [@heigold2025massive]
- [code](https://github.com/alexa/massive) --- Apache 2.0

## [alibaba/zvec](https://github.com/alibaba/zvec)
- [Home](https://zvec.org/en/)
- [Docs](https://zvec.org/en/docs/)

## [Crowdsourced high-quality UK and Ireland English Dialect speech data set](https://www.openslr.org/83/) [@demirsahin-etal-2020-open]
- Midlands, Northern, Southern English, Scottish, Welsh, Irish
- [Rainbow passage](https://www.dialectsarchive.com/the-rainbow-passage)

## [HuPER: A Human-Inspired Framework for Phonetic Perception](https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.01634)
- [arXiv](https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.01634)
- [code](https://github.com/HuPER29/HuPER) --- no licence
- [HuPER Recognizer (ARPAbet phone recognition)](https://huggingface.co/huper29/huper_recognizer)

## [Dysfluent WFST: A Framework for Zero-Shot Speech Dysfluency Transcription and Detection](https://www.isca-archive.org/interspeech_2025/guo25d_interspeech.html) [@guo25d_interspeech]
- [code](https://github.com/Berkeley-Speech-Group/DysfluentWFST) --- no licence
- [arXiv](https://arxiv.org/abs/2505.16351)

## [Coding Speech Through Vocal Tract Kinematics](https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/document/10759573) [@cho24_coding]
- [arXiv](https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.12998)
- [code](https://github.com/Berkeley-Speech-Group/Speech-Articulatory-Coding) --- MIT

## [LibriTTS: A Corpus Derived from LibriSpeech for Text-to-Speech](https://www.isca-archive.org/interspeech_2019/zen19_interspeech.html) [@zen19_interspeech]
- [arXiv](https://arxiv.org/abs/1904.02882)
- [OpenSLR](https://www.openslr.org/60/)

## [FLEURS: FEW-Shot Learning Evaluation of Universal Representations of Speech](https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/document/10023141) [@conneau2023fleurs]
- [arXiv](https://arxiv.org/abs/2205.12446)
- [Huggingface](https://huggingface.co/datasets/google/fleurs)

## [BEST-STD2.0: Balanced and Efficient Speech Tokenizer for Spoken Term Detection](https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.16395)

## [Scaling Open Discrete Audio Foundation Models with Interleaved Semantic, Acoustic, and Text Tokens](https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.16687)

## [facebookresearch/eb_jepa](https://github.com/facebookresearch/eb_jepa)

## [Language-Agnostic Speech Tokenizer for Spoken Term Detection with Efficient Retrieval](https://www.isca-archive.org/interspeech_2025/singh25d_interspeech.html) [@singh25d_interspeech]
- [word alignments](https://zenodo.org/records/15502222)
- [code](https://github.com/anupsingh15/LAST) --- MIT

## [BEST-STD: Bidirectional Mamba-Enhanced Speech Tokenization for Spoken Term Detection](https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/abstract/document/10889633) [@singh25beststd]
- [code](https://github.com/anupsingh15/BEST-STD) --- MIT

## [CC-G2PnP: Streaming Grapheme-to-Phoneme and prosody with Conformer-CTC for unsegmented languages](https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.17157)

## [Rethinking InfoNCE: How Many Negative Samples Do You Need?](https://www.ijcai.org/proceedings/2022/348) [@ijcai2022p348]

- "propose a probabilistic model to analyze the influence of the negative sampling ratio K on training sample informativeness."
- "design a training effectiveness function to measure the overall influence of training samples based on their informativeness"
- "estimate the optimal negative sampling ratio using the K value that maximizes the training effectiveness function"
- "further propose an adaptive negative sampling method that can dynamically adjust the negative sampling ratio to improve InfoNCE-based model training"

## [Investigation of Japanese PnG BERT Language Model in Text-to-Speech Synthesis for Pitch Accent Language](https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/document/9829304) [@yasuda2022pngbert]

- G2P + pitch
- BERT Base: 12 Transformer layers with 768 hidden
- trained up to 11.63 M steps with 20 batch size and learning rate with linear decay started from 5.0×10−5 with the Adam optimizer with L2 regularization
- randomly selected 25% words in a sentence for MLM:
  a. both graphemes and phonemes are masked for 48% random words
  b. character part is masked for 8% random words
  c. phoneme part is masked for 8% random words
  d. both characters and phonemes are randomly replaced with other phonemes or characters for 10% random words
- b and c not present in original PnG BERT

> Word embedding is mainly used to improve prosody of synthetic speech in TTS [28], [41]–​[46].

## [Combining multilingual resources to enhance end-to-end speech recognition systems for Scandinavian languages](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0167639325000366) [@MATEJU2025103221]
